title: Robot girlfriends, recursive AI agents, full AI research, Happy Horse: AI NEWS
summary: AI Search presents a video roundup of major AI announcements including Happy Horse, SenseNova U1, Talkie, Grok 4.3, and Vista 4D. The segment covers emerging tools spanning robotics, multi-agent systems, video generation, and large language models. The video showcases diverse AI capabilities from computer vision to multimodal agent reasoning.
writeup: A weekly AI roundup spanning video generation, motion capture, open-source models, and multi-agent architecture. The through-line is a tension between benchmark claims and real-world performance: Hap.ai (Happy Horse) ranks first on Artificial Analysis for text-to-video yet fails basic physics and prompt-following tests, while Seed Dance 2.0 handles the same prompts cleanly. Alongside the skepticism, the episode introduces recursive multi-agent systems, which let agents collaborate in latent space rather than text, yielding a 2.4x to 4x speedup, 75% fewer tokens, and over 8% accuracy gains on benchmarks.
key_points: Hap.ai (Happy Horse) by Alibaba ranks roughly 100 points above Seed Dance 2.0 on the Artificial Analysis text-to-video leaderboard, yet hands-on tests showed broken physics and poor prompt adherence, raising serious doubts about the independence or methodology of that leaderboard.

OmniShot Cut is a 164 MB model trained on 2.5 million internet videos and 300,000 synthetic clips with 11 million labeled transitions; it identifies exact cut timestamps and classifies transition types (hard cut, dissolve, fade, slide, cross zoom) and is available as a free Hugging Face Space.

Mocap Anything v2 replaces the traditional two-step joint-detection plus inverse-kinematics pipeline with a single end-to-end model that directly predicts joint rotations, eliminating the unlearnable IK step that caused jitter and unnatural spinning in v1.

Recursive multi-agent systems keep agents in latent space throughout reasoning, only decoding text at the final output step; measured results show a 2.4x to 4x speedup, 75% token reduction, and over 8% accuracy improvement, with performance continuing to rise as more loops are added rather than plateauing like text-based chains.

Link 2.6 Flash from Inclusion AI (another Alibaba spinoff) uses a mixture-of-experts architecture with 104 billion total parameters but only 7.4 billion active per forward pass, making it fast at long context lengths relative to comparably sized dense models.

ZAnime is a full fine-tune (not a LoRA) of the Zimage Base open-source image model on anime data, released in FP8 (6 GB) and GGUF variants plus a distilled four-step version that generates images in seconds on consumer GPUs.
